description: Conversation starts with explicit agreement not to schedule anything, then pivots to a mandatory legal contract review on Thursday that cannot be avoided. The final confirmed event overrides the earlier anti-scheduling sentiment.

events.title: Contract Review
events.reasoning: After agreeing to avoid new meetings, Alice remembers a mandatory legal contract review on Thursday at 4 PM in Conference Room B. Dave confirms attendance and Alice says she will send the invite. This overrides the earlier anti-scheduling statements.
events.assumptions: Thursday = Feb 26; 30 minutes max interpreted as 30-minute duration

mock_llm_response.summary: One event extracted: a legal contract review on Thursday Feb 26 at 4:00 PM in Conference Room B. The earlier 'no more meetings' sentiment is superseded by the confirmed mandatory event.

notes: Negation then real pattern. The model must understand that the earlier anti-scheduling statements apply to optional meetings, but the mandatory legal meeting is explicitly carved out and confirmed by both parties. Alice's statement 'I'll send the invite now' seals it.
